articles.title: The Cover
articles.author: Ruby Johnson
articles.complete_text:
"The Bride" is the title of the cover picture of this issue of the Magazine. When the new-green of spring has changed from its unbelievable freshness to the full-leafed assurance of summer, roses bloom in warm beauty, scenting June nights with their fragrance. The world awakens again to love. Charming brides pledge their love in marriage; noble minds are linked through spiritual ceremonies. The joy of a bride is perhaps the dreamed-of happiness which surpasses all previous joys of her life. When solemnized in the House of the Lord, hers is the promise of lasting love and eternal progression.

"The Bride," the cover picture, is from a drawing by Evan Jensen. The model is the artist's wife, Beth Boggess Jensen.
articles.notes: This is a substantive article explaining the cover art with spiritual commentary on marriage and temple ceremonies, authored by Ruby Johnson

articles.title: The Songster
articles.author: Anna H. Michie
articles.complete_text:
Out in the apple tree sits a gay songster
Tilting so lightly among the sweet blooms,
Gladness and joy he is sowing about him,
Cheering the world with his full-throated tunes.

Now, do I see why his song is so joyous,
Why he is singing with such lusty pride;
In their new nest in the branches close by him,
Happily listening, sits his young bride.

Sing, little warbler, your joy is supernal,
Cheer the whole world with your song of true love;
Sadness departs on the wings of your music,
Up soar my spirits to blue skies above.
articles.notes: Complete poem by Anna H. Michie, clearly formatted in stanzas with consistent meter and rhyme scheme

articles.title: Let Us Sing to Love
articles.author: Inez Ruth Stevens
articles.complete_text:
Let us sing to love which is from God,
For love is found in all the universe.
Love, like the still waters, is deep;
It is refreshing to the weary traveler.

Love has the strength of the mountains
And endures even as they.
The heart is uplifted by it as a bird that soars above the mountain, singing.

Love has the straight growth of a tree reaching for sunshine;
It bears fruit in rich abundance.

Love has the brightness and warmth of the sun,
The glory of its rising,
And the quiet of its setting.

Love is the shadow of a tree at noon-day
And a welcome shelter in storm.

Nowhere can be found anything like love
For it has the beauty of a moonlit valley,
And it is a bright star;
It has the delicacy of a newly opened bud,
And it fills the soul as the perfume of a rare flower.

It is wildly beating as the high-tossed waves of the sea
And as calm as a desert evening
With whisperings and the caress of gentle breezes.

In love
There are heights unclimbed,
There are depths unsounded,
There is vastness unmeasured,
And there is the intimate closeness of the drop of dew hidden in the flower's heart.

Let us sing all together in praise of love,
For love is wonderful past understanding.
It is a choice gift from God.
articles.notes: Complete spiritual poem by Inez Ruth Stevens about divine love, with clear poetic structure and religious themes
